verdict: 2026-07-13 13:19:24 PDT — NOT merge-ready. The server teardown and dependency shape remain sound, and the two architecture SVGs are byte-identical. However, the synchronized diagram still overstates the server’s no-data guarantee, and several live documents retain current-sounding persistence/app-context language.

findings.title: Synchronized SVGs still overclaim server data handling
findings.claim: Both now-identical diagrams say the server “Stores no user data,” while the unchanged `/api/log` endpoint records arbitrary submitted JSON bodies up to 100 KB and Railway retains those logs. The legend’s “nothing is stored on our server” at line 101 reinforces the same categorical claim. This contradicts the qualified logging posture established in Privacy, README, and the policies.
findings.alternative: State the narrow invariant: the server stores no presenter configuration, rates, names, or meeting history. Explicitly acknowledge operational request and client-diagnostic logs, or link the diagram to the Privacy Policy for their qualified handling.
findings.win: Eliminates two false public/security claims in both diagram copies while preserving their byte-identical synchronization and requiring no code change.

findings.title: Live-doc reconciliation still leaves persistence and deleted app-context language
findings.claim: README says “saved settings” work on older clients, the public documentation says “saved figures” work regardless of version, Incident Response lists exposure of “stored configuration” as a current incident example, and Security classifies “OAuth tokens / app context” as current data. The product now saves no settings/configuration, and the app-context identity path was deleted. These are live, current-facing documents rather than the clearly archived roadmap or historical review records.
findings.alternative: Replace “saved settings/figures” with “side-panel controls,” update the incident example to operational logs or credentials, and remove “app context” from the current data classification while retaining OAuth-token handling.
findings.win: Removes four residual references that can make operators, users, or reviewers infer persistence or deleted identity machinery still exists.
